title: Dreamy Canadian Baby Squid Chowder
description: A comforting Canadian dish featuring baby squid and kombu-cold-extract, this chowder is nutty and aerated, perfect for a cold winter's night, and is also FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the garlic-infused oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and celery and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the potatoes, chicken broth, and kombu-cold-extract, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Add the baby squid and cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the squid is opaque and cooked through.
Stir in the coconut milk and cook for 1 minute, or until the chowder is heated through.
Season the chowder with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the chowder hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
